nyheter.title: Epic Fury has Navy rethinking carrier deployment tempo
nyheter.summary: The record-breaking deployment of the USS Gerald R. Ford has reopened the debate about how the Navy should structure deployments and time at home.

nyheter.summary:
The UK is re-deploying its Type 45 air-defence destroyer HMS Dragon to the Middle East, to pre-position the ship in preparation for any potential multinational mission to secure Strait of Hormuz commercial shipping transits. Such a mission would be intended to take place when the current conflict in the Gulf region has concluded. “The pre-positioning ...

In Vladivostok, Russia The Russian Navy Pacific Fleet task group arrived Thursday in Vladivostok, Russia, according to a Russian Navy Pacific Fleet news release. The task group included submarine RFS Petropavlovsk-Kamchatsky (B-274), corvette RFS Gromkiy (335) and tugboat Andrei Stepanov. nyheter.title: Future Aircraft Carrier Doris Miller Delayed by 2 Years
nyheter.summary: One of the Navy’s Ford-class aircraft carriers is delayed by two years, stretching the schedule to build the future USS Doris Miller (CVN-81) out to 15 years, USNI News has learned. Carrier Doris Miller will now deliver to the Navy in February of 2034, according to the service’s Fiscal Year 2027 budget justification books. Last year’s books projected the ship’s delivery in February of 2032. “The CVN-81 delivery date shifted from February 2032 to February 2034 due to shipbuilder construction foot

nyheter.title: Strait of Hormuz Shipping in State of Confusion as Industry Watches U.S., Iranian Actions
nyheter.summary: Three Navy destroyers came under fire from Iran as they transited the Strait of Hormuz as commercial shipping traffic remains at an almost standstill. Iranian forces sent missiles, drones and small boats after USS Mason (DDG-87), USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115) and USS Truxton (DDG-103) as the three warships headed toward the Gulf of Oman, according to U.S. Central Command news release. nyheter.title: GAO Report on Mariner Training
nyheter.summary: Why GAO Did This Study The maritime industry relies on mariners to work on vessels carrying goods and passengers domestically and internationally. However, industry stakeholders have raised concerns about a mariner shortage. To work in the industry, mariners often take courses through a training institution. nyheter.title: French Carrier Strike Group Now Operating in the Middle East
nyheter.summary: The French Carrier Strike Group transited the Suez Canal on Wednesday en route to the Red Sea, as part of a multinational naval coalition that seeks to restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. “The multinational mission that France and the United Kingdom have set up can help restore confidence among shipowners and insurers. nyheter.title: Cahill Nominated to Lead Naval Surface Force, Cash Nominated as Navy’s Top Chaplain
nyheter.summary: President Donald Trump nominated Rear Adm. Joe Cahill to serve as the next commander of Naval Surface Force, the Pentagon announced Tuesday. Cahill, who is the current commander of Naval Surface Force Atlantic in Norfolk, Va., will be promoted to vice admiral if confirmed as the next SWO Boss. A career surface warfare officer, Cahill has served on several destroyers, Ticonderoga-class guided-missile cruiser USS Bunker Hill (CG-52) and Kitty Hawk-class aircraft carrier USS Constellation (CV-64),

nyheter.title: Carrier USS Gerald R. Ford is Now in the Atlantic
nyheter.summary: Aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) is now operating in the Atlantic Ocean after sailing through the Strait of Gibraltar on Wednesday, according to ship spotters. As of Wednesday, Ford has been deployed for 315 days, the longest carrier strike group deployment since the end of the Cold War. Since the start of the Vietnam War, only USS Coral Sea (CVA-43) in 1964 and USS Midway (CVA-41) in 1972 have deployed longer for national tasking. Ford, the Navy’s newest carrier, was initially

nyheter.title: ‘Littoral Deep Battle’
nyheter.summary: LAOAG, Philippines — Target drones resembling Chinese amphibious infantry fighting vehicles approached the La Paz Sand Dunes at a steady pace. In the next few hours, a combined force of American, Philippine, Canadian and Japanese troops took turns tearing into the simulated beach landing, trialing a plan devised by the U.S. Army to defeat amphibious invasions. Once the gunfire and smoke subsided, American, Japanese and Philippine counterparts gathered on the beach, culminating their combined war

nyheter.title: U.S. Tests Using Drones to Track, Target Suspected Narco Boats
nyheter.summary: U.S. 4th Fleet and U.S. Naval Southern Command recently tested the use of drones and artificial intelligence to track and target suspected narco boats and counter transnational organized crime. The drill, conducted in the waters off Key West, Fla., was part of this year’s iteration of the Navy’s annual Fleet Experimentation (FLEX) exercise, which spanned April 24-30, according to a Navy news release. nyheter.title: Royal Navy Plans to Use Hybrid Fleet to Keep Pace with Russia in High North
nyheter.summary: The Royal Navy will use its hybrid fleet, enhanced with autonomous and uncrewed vessels, to keep pace with Russia in the High North and North Atlantic, according to the service’s transformation plan. First Sea Lord Gen. Gywn Jenkins, head of the Royal Navy, outlined the service’s Hybrid Navy transformation plan last week in a Wednesday speech at the Royal United Services Institute in London. nyheter.title: Carrier USS Gerald R. Ford Operating in the Mediterranean Sea
nyheter.summary: Aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) is now operating in the Mediterranean after leaving the Red Sea last week, USNI News has learned. Ford transited the Suez Canal last week, a defense official confirmed to USNI News. The carrier is expected to sail home to Naval Station Norfolk, Va., after breaking the post-Cold War deployment record last month, USNI News reported at the time. rov_teknologi.title: First Columbia-class Sub Tracking to 2028 Delivery, General Dynamics Says
